communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems and Methods
communities.summary: This community encompasses the core techniques, models, and applications of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which combines neural retrievers with language model generators to ground outputs in external knowledge and reduce hallucinations. It covers retrieval strategies (dense, hybrid, reranking), fusion approaches, evaluation metrics, datasets across domains (financial, biomedical), and addresses key challenges like knowledge updates and domain adaptation in LLM-based systems.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems and Enhancements
communities.summary: This community focuses on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) as a paradigm for enhancing large language models by integrating external knowledge sources to reduce hallucinations and improve factuality. The papers collectively address RAG architectures, retrieval strategies, reranking methods, multimodal extensions, robustness improvements, and comprehensive benchmarking across diverse domains.

communities.title: Context Compression and Robustness in RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods and frameworks for improving RAG system efficiency and reliability through context compression techniques (both soft and hard prompting approaches), robustness mechanisms against retrieval defects, and evaluation/monitoring tools. The entities unite around enhancing RAG performance by reducing computational overhead while maintaining quality and handling imperfect retrieval scenarios.

communities.title: Neural Retrieval and Ranking for Open-Domain Question Answering
communities.summary: This community encompasses neural retrieval methods and dense embedding approaches that power modern RAG systems, including Dense Passage Retrievers and learning-to-hash techniques for efficient passage encoding. It covers instruction-tuned LLM frameworks like RankRAG that jointly optimize context ranking and answer generation, evaluated on knowledge-intensive benchmarks such as Natural Questions, TriviaQA, and PopQA.

communities.title: Active Retrieval and Citation in RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods and evaluation frameworks for improving retrieval-augmented generation through active retrieval decisions, citation quality, and long-form question answering. It includes datasets (ALCE, QAMPARI, ELI5, StrategyQA) and techniques (FLARE, interactive retrieval, passage reranking) designed to enhance when and what language models retrieve, along with metrics for evaluating citation accuracy and fluency in generated answers.

communities.title: Adaptive and Optimized RAG for Complex Question Answering
communities.summary: This community unites methods and techniques for enhancing retrieval-augmented generation systems to handle complex, multi-hop question answering tasks through adaptive retrieval strategies, evidence optimization, and reinforcement learning-based feedback mechanisms. The focus spans from dynamic routing and boundary-aware selection to compression techniques and iterative retrieval approaches, all evaluated on open-domain QA benchmarks using various language models.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Language Models and Training Methods
communities.summary: This community encompasses retrieval-augmented language modeling approaches that enhance LLMs by integrating external retrieval components, including architectural frameworks (RETRO, REALM, REPLUG), training methodologies (LM-supervised retrieval, RA-DIT, SAIL), and practical implementations with various model scales and evaluation benchmarks. The common thread unites techniques for adapting retrievers to language model supervision, combining off-the-shelf LMs with retrieval systems, and measuring improvements in language understanding and generation tasks.

communities.title: Graph-Based and Hybrid Retrieval for Knowledge-Intensive QA
communities.summary: This community encompasses structured knowledge representation approaches (knowledge graphs, graph-based indexing) combined with neural retrieval methods (cross-encoders, vector databases) for improving question answering and summarization tasks. The entities highlight the evolution from parametric language models to retrieval-augmented systems that leverage both graph structures and vector embeddings, with GraphRAG and HybridRAG representing modern hybrid approaches for complex information retrieval and generation.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems and Components
communities.summary: This community encompasses RAG architectures, retrieval and reranking methods, language models serving as readers/rerankers, and benchmark datasets spanning multiple domains. The entities represent the full pipeline of RAG systems—from retrieval (DPR, BGE) through reranking (monoT5, G-RAG) to reading comprehension (T5, Gemini, GPT-4o)—with evaluation on diverse QA tasks including open-domain, multi-hop, and scientific document understanding.

communities.title: RAG System Development, Evaluation, and Component Infrastructure
communities.summary: This community encompasses frameworks and tools for building and evaluating RAG systems, including EncouRAGe's modular architecture for system development, RAGAS for comprehensive evaluation combining LLM-based and classical NLP metrics, and supporting components like hybrid retrieval methods and vector store implementations. These entities collectively address the full lifecycle of RAG system creation, from component selection and integration to rigorous performance assessment using both traditional metrics and modern LLM-as-a-Judge approaches.

communities.title: Graph-Based Retrieval and Entity-Centric Knowledge Organization
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods that enhance RAG systems by structuring knowledge as graphs of entities and their relationships, utilizing techniques like entity extraction, semantic linking, and graph-based ranking algorithms. These approaches leverage graph traversal, community detection, and importance aggregation to enable more precise multi-hop retrieval and hierarchical knowledge organization.

communities.title: Agentic Systems for Code Generation and Text Optimization
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods and systems that enable autonomous agents to iteratively improve code and text artifacts through feedback loops, retrieval, and reflection. The research focuses on agentic coding tasks, context engineering for LLM applications, and text optimization techniques that leverage prior solutions and rollout traces to achieve state-of-the-art performance on challenging benchmarks including mathematical reasoning and programming tasks.

communities.title: Prompting and Fine-tuning Techniques for Language Models
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods for adapting and guiding language models through prompting strategies (chain-of-thought, zero-shot reasoning, prompt engineering) and parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches (prefix-tuning, instruction-tuning). These techniques enable large language models to perform reasoning tasks and follow instructions more effectively, with applications to improving RAG robustness through better utilization of retrieved content.

communities.title: Iterative Retrieval-Generation Methods for Complex Reasoning
communities.summary: This community encompasses techniques that interleave retrieval and generation processes to tackle multi-step reasoning tasks. These methods enhance traditional RAG by using model-generated hypotheses or intermediate outputs to refine retrieval, enabling better performance on commonsense reasoning, multi-hop QA, and fact verification tasks.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Formal Theorem Proving in Lean
communities.summary: This community centers on automated theorem proving in the Lean proof assistant, leveraging retrieval-augmented generation to solve the premise selection bottleneck. It encompasses datasets like LeanDojo Benchmark and methods like ReProver that combine neural language models with retrieval mechanisms to enable programmatic formal proof generation at scale.

communities.title: RAG Pipeline Development and Retrieval Infrastructure
communities.summary: This community encompasses frameworks, libraries, and methods for building, evaluating, and optimizing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems. It unites declarative platforms for composing multi-stage IR architectures (PyTerrier, PyTerrier-RAG, FlashRAG) with core retrieval techniques spanning learned sparse models (SPLADE), dense retrievers (ColBERT), and efficient similarity search backends (FAISS, HNSW), alongside reasoning-enhanced RAG approaches (IRCoT).

communities.title: Multimodal RAG: Integrating Visual Evidence with Language Models
communities.summary: This community encompasses datasets and methods that extend RAG systems beyond text to incorporate visual information, enabling multimodal language models to retrieve and reason over both images and text. The benchmarks (Visual-RAG, InfoSeek, MRAG-Bench, WebQA) standardize evaluation of systems that perform text-to-image retrieval and visual evidence extraction to answer knowledge-intensive questions grounded in visual content.
